original_image_tags: A stamped ink box from the FBI - Richmond field office, dated May 17, 1949.

elements.description: A newspaper clipping with the headline 'Remember Flying Saucers? Well Looks Like Flying Seegars Now' from the Bristol Herald Courier, Bristol, VA, dated 5/4/49. The clipping contains typed body text reporting on sightings of a 'long, cigar-shaped object' and a 'flaming object' in Kingsport, Christiansburg, Roanoke, and Pulaski. It describes the object as a 'whitish, glistening mass, long and cylindrical like a big cigar' and mentions it traveling northeast at about 30,000 feet. The clipping is yellowed and has a handwritten date '5/14/49' at the bottom left.

A rectangular ink stamp in the lower right area of the page. The text inside the stamp reads 'FBI - RICHMOND' on the first line and 'MAY 1 7 1949' on the second line. Below the stamp, there is a handwritten red number '94-259' and, further down, a red handwritten enclosure number '62 - 83894 - 178'. The word 'ENCLOSURE' is also handwritten in red ink above this final number.

judge.reason: Description A describes only a single stamp element, while Description B describes two distinct visual elements (a newspaper clipping illustration and a stamp) with contradictory and mutually exclusive content about the page's images.
